reviews.review_text: Just go play Terraria. The developer/publisher is a massive piece of ♥♥♥♥ (so bad that ConcernedApe bought the rights to his own game back so he wouldn't be kicked off of his own IP) and the game is not good, it's not what was promised, the developer abandoned it in an unfinished state, it was developed using exploited free labor, and the game is almost nothing like what this page presents. Go spend your money on something better.

In a universe of infinite procedurally generated fetch quests, why do I feel so alone?

The lore drops aspire to the level of second-rate fanfiction. The main storyline is stuck several levels below that, being both excruciatingly boring and poorly written (confusing 'reap' and 'wreak', etc.).

The appeal to this game for me was in the impression that you could create your own character and tell your own story. However, that promise is not realised. Progress is linear and exploration consists of the same few components in each category (weaponry, biomes, quests, dungeons, creatures, etc.) in various permutations. The game tells you you are exploring the vastness of space, but in effect you are walking round in circles. There is a dearth of original content and what is there is poorly designed and falls within a narrow and generally unpleasant aesthetic range. 

For designing characters and collecting 'their' gear, I'll stick with Terraria: there is no contest.

The community is strong, which is nice to see. Two themes emerge:

(1) The exploitation of interns in the development process of the game was regrettable.

(2) The game had a lot of potential that for various reasons not unrelated to (1) was never realised.

I am ignorant of (1) beyond the fact that it is an issue that is often brought up and lamented. It is something I wish I had been aware of prior to purchase.

I am unconvinced of (2). I think this game shows signs of being poorly planned from the get-go. I suspect the developers backed themselves into a corner with the reliance on procedural generation, which in the absence of a disciplined and creative planning process failed to produce diverse in-game experiences. 

Overall, it seems to me that this was always destined to be a game with a lot of Stuff in it and not much to say. 

Despite the above, this game might well be for you if you: really like guns and shoot-outs, find throwing bombs at people trying to shoot you thrilling, enjoy playing at cops and robbers (heaven help us), and/or are looking for a bit of a cozy break from your favourite FPS.

I think the races are fun. You can play instruments! LIke the zither! Building seems fairly well implemented.
one of the only games i come back to over the years. great for a parent and a kid. amazing 2player adventures

Absolutely worth experiencing, but I wouldn't buy the game at full price these days.

The gameplay is fun, the exploration is good, the pixel graphics and worldbuilding is cute, and the soundtrack is unforgettable. I would be over the moon if this game received a good sequel.

It still has a decently active modding community that adds wide array of features and stuff to the game, it had a rough development and been basically abandoned but I definitely recommend it next time it is on sale definitely a good steam sale grab. It is one of the more underrated space games even with its overpromises and flaws.
I loved pixel games: used to torrent old beta versions of starbound and now here i am. If u love stardewvalley terraria and those sort of game then Starbound is the game for u!

This game has brought so much joy into my life. The music, artstyle, story, and gameplay are so incredible and blend together to curate a relaxing and engaging experience. The modding community is so wonderful and honestly make the game perfect.

This game is like Terraria. but in SPACE! unlike Terraria tho, you can have a ♥♥♥♥ ton of workshop mods that add a metric ♥♥♥♥ TON of content, from guns, to armor, to ship parts to music and instruments. you won't get bored, and will have HOURS of playtime!

It's a curious, often frustrating experience. The game certainly feels under-cooked when you go in vanilla, but the modding scene is likely so large and robust for that very reason. That being said, there are certain things about it that you can't even get from other games, and the vast, sometimes boring cosmos is no doubt made up for by the depth of the character OF said cosmos. 
The game does a wonderful job with its premise, really making you feel like a straggling nomad who just lost everything, lost out in the middle of deep space.
Many games that are boring or even bad can be ignored. Starbound, at its base, kinda sucks, but that just makes me WANT to try my hardest to have fun with it, instead of just turn it away and view it as a waste of time or money.

I highly recommend giving this game the attention that its devs didn't, hopefully it can see a second life in the hands of the players.

it’s an interesting game. I really liked the progression system and the weapon system. The only issue is the repetition in the main storyline — like it keeps asking you to go scan an area, then fight a boss, then scan again, then fight another boss. So the missions feel kinda repetitive, but overall it’s still a good game.
don't support unpaid child labor; play a different game.

I'm late to this game and I wish I would've discovered it sooner. I played through the entire game vanilla before I modded, and honest review on both: 
Vanilla, it's good, I know some people feel it's unfinished, but as someone who doesn't know the history behind the game outside of the game itself it feels like a completed game and it has good flow/pacing. You can continue to explore after you complete the full storyline which is really nice, especially for builders. 
Modded: It feels like modded minecraft and terraria had a kid. I'm doing Frackin' and because I'm so late to the ballgame it's not buggy (love this) and there's very few things missing. I love the mod commitment to the love of the game, there's so much in depth lore, but there is also SOOOO much to do. I love building and the addition of all the cool races and their furniture and planets and such, and that there was some extended stuff onto the base game stories. Really nice to bring life into the game after you've done all you felt you could do in vanilla. 
I'd recommend this game for sure, and I hope that they keep it alive.
Starbound is such an amazing blend of sandbox and adventure game. No matter what I'm in the mood to do, I can jet off to a planet and do it - quest, build, mine, catch cute little critters.

the idea of the game is great the way it was done was ok. the main bad thing about it is that teens who made the game were underpaid. But the game in a nutshell is no mans sky mixed with starfield and terraria. the story i havent completed but from what people say its not that good. i would recommend if your a explorer and fighter also if you liek the building from terriara the building on this one is very simlar

This game provides hours of potential play but without the community it is a mile wide but inch deep endeavor. the mods and efforts put in after playing the base game that makes things special here. this game is very much a storytelling game but only under the pretense that you put in efforts to find the next location or you lose the momentum that gives this game that potential of fun, mods or fan efforts be damned.
If you like terraria you'll probably like this game too, if not more. Notably, Starbound is more about exploration and terraria is more about combat, **in my opinion,** so do with that what you will.

2D sandbox and a space exploration game combined.
Definitely a game I can play for days, probably even weeks without getting tired of it, even just playing solo
(Whatever, go my 8-hour play sessions)
Only downside is that occasionally, especially when streaming for friends, the game does have pretty bad lag spikes, but they only last for about 5 seconds, so its never too bad
[Also feeds my current fixation on Avali, I love these silly things.(you should play with the mods for 'em)]
Honestly no idea why so many people say this game is worse than starbound, its far better, especially when just adding one mod, Frackin Universe.
It's just not... [i]fun[/i].  It could have been an incredible game, but it's just kinda boring somehow.

So, after 1,000 hours, and several up and down experiences, I've come to the conclusion this game isn't bad, just underappreciated. From what I've done with my research, this game was "choked" through development.

Don't get me wrong, it's a good game for what it is, but if you look at starbounder (the website), there's many many many items and events in the game you'll just never run into, from things like the crystal furniture set, the big red bow, curious painting, deck chair, cupid set, pizza, soldier set, among so many other things, so many other things.... For all the fun this game could have been, it makes no sense to have pages upon pages of disabled content, that COULD be enjoyed by the players. It reminds me of the saying; "Of all sad words of tongue or pen, the saddest are these, It might have been." -John Greenleaf

So, the way it looks to me, the dev team was forcibly removed from the Starbound as an ongoing project after the controversy of 2019. For clarity, in 2019, former Starbound writer Damon Reece publicly stated on Twitter that he had worked hundreds of unpaid hours on the game. This was confirmed by Toby Fox (creator of Undertale, who did graphical work for Starbound), and brought forth evidence of a hostile environment created by then CEO of Chucklefish. -- All of this is public knowledge at this point, able to be found on any number of search engine.

In comparison to other Chucklefish Games: Stardew Valley and Witchbrook, it's no surprise Starbound is considered lost, and abandoned after the 1.4.x.x updates.

Then there the matter of workshop mods, lots of mods, and many of them are "will they/won't they" when it comes to working. Several of which ultimately change the game, and not always in ways that are safe for your universe or character files (often unsafe, and makes your characters henceforth unplayable without the mods, which is just bad design and sloppy practice; make backups of your character and universe files, always)

That said, it doesn't take away from what the game is, at this point. If you look past the shadows, you look past the allegations, the dubious news, and even considering the internet will never forget the shadow that this game came from, it nevertheless amounted to something. Don't think of the antics of the CEO (not giving them a pass, just saying, the internet will ALWAYS remember), don't think of the cracks in the code, or the many mods that may try to turn the game into something it was never supposed to be.

Think of what it is out of the box: think of the people who worked and collaborated to make the game what it is, think of it as where Damon Reece and Toby Fox (among many others who I can't even begin to list) got their first steps. Think of those who put hours of work and labor into the game, and all worked together to breathe life into something, that turned out beautiful. Think of those who grew from the experience, or who contributed freely, who WANTED to help make something that felt alive, and worth getting into. To ignore this game, in a sort-of way, is to neglect that even though this game came from a dark place, it was made into something wonderful by those among the dev team, and those who contributed from the community, and still do so today because a piece of their work and their effort went into breathing life into a world that is there for YOU to enjoy.

With this having been put out there, Starbound is not a bad game, it's a slow starter for sure, but once you get going, you discover an open universe to explore. A story about an ancient conflict, mysteries lost in space, several easter eggs to sci-fi and space themed media from the 80s, a charming soundtrack, and worlds ranging from wonderful and fantastical to weird or scary. 

1,000 hours, exploring, discovering, replaying... I've tried to play with online groups, but it always falls apart, someone's schedule doesn't work, someone abandons the playthrough or decides they don't get along with XYZ, or the game where one person wants to be the center of attention to the point other players get neglected. I've concluded this game is best enjoyed solo, or with a SMALL group of friends. 

But... It is worth playing... It's a slow one, still, it's endearing and you can feel the hints of love in the design. There were people who wanted to use this game to make money, and there are those who just wanted to breathe life into it; this game shows art imitates life, an abyssal devourer, and a cosmic guardian. 

Some of the pros are the worlds, and exploration, the many randomly generating aliens and items that a player will find creating an infinite number of possible treasures, and all kinds of worlds to enjoy, and a colorful cast of NPC friends (like Nuru, Koichi or the Baron) 
Some of the cons include the inability to adjust your character after creation (there are no salons, tanning booths, barbers or hair stylists in space?), the tediousness of upgrading your mech (not only do you have to find the blueprints, you also need to find the material to make the parts of said blueprints), or literal world-destroying environmental effects (From acid rain to arbitrary meteor striking and destroying you and/or everything around you, in later planets)

Still, once you LEARN the ins and outs of the game, what's dangerous, what to do when there are certain places you have to go, it becomes easier. You can build a crew, bring along an alien pet, you can find all kinds of gear (or craft some yourself), and there are ways to avoid dangers, once you understand them (eg: using a breathing pack, so you don't suffocate)

The universe is full of mystery, and there are cosmic disasters to be confronted! Will you destroy planets with your matter manipulator, or use it to create something to stand against the darkness of the infinite universal abyss! It's up to you to decide.
fantastic game!  I've been playing off and on for years, and even 'beat' it once.  Definitely one you should mod!

I started playing this game in very early access. Back when you could only fuel your ship with Coal and you had to build an SOS beacon to summon Dreadwing. I've watched this game grow and evolve over the years and I've absolutely loved every step of its development. Watching the little sandbox survival game develop into a full blown story and an expansive universe is utterly beautiful. 

The OST, the Gameplay, The Story, I love it all. It is a cozy and beautiful game about exploring the universe and even years into playing it, its still fun to go back every once in a while to explore some more.

This is the perfect game for someone who wants something comfortable to play with just the right amount of excitement. Plus with the extensive modding community, you can have whatever playstyle you want. Genuinely a 10/10

the mods are really fun, and i will say this game is great if you like dressing up your character and building houses with lots of rooms. HOWEVER. the game is apparently horribly optimized and will even heat up a powerful computer. there are some things you can do to help that, like downloading mods that help with optimization or disabling vsync, but at the end of the day its an issue with the game

This is a Fantastic game. From taking on the secrets of the universe as a plant man or becoming a scummy real estate agent as a regular guy, this game has a lot to do. Some of my favourite mechanics are just assembling your crew and building new bases on far away planets in systems that have a lot of potential. The only valid criticism i have for this game is that the start of the game up until after that fuel mission on that one moon is pretty annoying to do, but even then, its actually fairly enjoyable the first few times round. Anyways totally recommend to all those wanting a wacky adventure through the cosmos, totally worth the £12.99

I know that Chucklefish has moved on from here, and I know that you guys are making/have made a lot of cool games since this game came out, but it's not just me begging you here. This game has so much more traction than the rest because the idea was awesome and the game was really great. Give us Starbound II. Buy out Frackin' if you want to and just make us one whole game that is amazing once again. I know Witchbrook will be great and it's coming too, but this title deserves a sequel, you have most of the assets you need and the time is ripe!
Genuinely a really calming experience and beautiful game! Plus one of the best soundtracks doesn't hurt.

i have hundreds of hours on this game on different platforms and though it makes me sad to think of what this game could have been with proper development conditions and a bit more love i can appreciate its current state especially with a highly dedicated modding community. this is one of my favorite "oc creator" games and exploration can get a bit repetitive, but it's just like sims in the respect that you have to come up with a story yourself and that comes naturally with the rich lore of the world and fun character dialogue. would recommend if you want a more character centric sandbox and super recommend if you can drag your reluctant friends into it (hint it's not just "space terraria!")
if you're looking for a grand and wonderful story with deep exploration this is not for you. and honestly i can't play it without mods anymore ;,)

Really enjoyable experience I've kept coming back to over and over again. I'm a big fan of the race designs and overall feel, and mod support keeps it feeling fresh. Only real issue I've had over my time with the game is the performance issues even with my current setup, which is strange.

I'm also not super fond of the story/main questline? I feel like it's kinda out of place for a sandbox game such as this (and I may have just gotten bored of it after starting new saves over and over again, but story disabler mods exist)

Game is fun, with friends, game was also left in a you-have-to-severely-mod-this-game state because of something with the company, but the game and its idea left behind I particularly enjoy. Choose wisely how much time you wish to invest in this game.

It's fun, but it's obviously not Terraria 2.0, which a lot of people were hoping it would be initially. Just doesn't scratch that same itch. I would treat it more as a fun story game than a sandbox game like Terraria. The main issue is with all these planets you can explore, nowhere truly feels like home. You feel like a menace going from world to world sucking all the resources out of each planet and leaving them as wastelands.
Very complicated game, but very fun! I'd recommend playing this game modded over just base game though

you'll like this game if you like terraria and no mans sky, its unique but similar to those games. also its about as similar to terraria as terraria is to minecraft

buy when its on sale, because the devs didn't pay most of their employees.

Closer in game feel to No Man's Sky than Terraria. This was also the game I based my opinion of NMS on, I knew it was going to fail just because of the flaws I saw in this game. Fell lockstep into many of the same problems.

I dislike the unique split inventory requiring keybinds for certain tools which have multiple modes. Obtuse.

Generally empty and doesn't reward building on planets, or really doing anything other than finding whatever dungeon is there. the building mechanics are largely ignored in the grand scheme of things as the creative aspect would ruin many of the games challenges.
The winter beta was peak, has one of my favorite soundtracks ever. Unfortunately I can only recommend people play modded. Frackin Universe 10/10

First thing first, shout out to the mod community to make this remotely playable in its current state.

But the reality is even with thousands of mod this game is fundamentally... boring, with everything so generic and chore to play through...

It definitely strives to be a calmer alternative to similar 2D sandbox games (specifically one starting with T that maybe only a few people have heard of...). You don't quite get the 'vastness' of space which you might expect from a game called Starbound, but you do absolutely get that sense of tranquility being the lone soul on a new frontier (until you get bombarded by gunshots from a nearby camp). The story is sufficable but easy, although it's not the game's focus. If you're just beelining the narrative (which I'll be honest was the extent of my experience for quite a long time), then you're really not getting the full experience.

I'll be completely honest, this game is mainly good with mods. The base game has a very slow progression and can feel rather tedious at times, so please go through the Steam Workshop and download some stuff. Also the mods allow me to play as an Eevee, so that's a huge win for me.
Other than that I put 72 hours into the game within 3 weeks so obvious I like it lol
It's like knock-off Terraria in space,  with unique planets to explore

Have you ever played Terraria?
Played some No Man's Sky, perhaps?
Well, Starbound is the lovechild between those two that got abandoned on a dark, rainy night.
Abandoned in the sense that... The last substancial update was [b] [i] before the pandemic. [/i] [/b]
Then again, the devs considered it “finished”, so who am I to judge.
[spoiler] I guess the founder/director of Chucklefish ran out of underaged “volunteers”. [/spoiler]

As stated, Starbound looks and plays like Terraria, but has the athmosphere and setting of NMS.
Star systems and planets are procedurally generated, all of which containing randomly generated biomes, sometimes with structures... Even the weapons and creatures you find there are procedurally generated. There are some survival aspects, mainly just being hungry sometimes, but i never bothered with any real “survival” stuff.
You can also play as 6 different races who have their own themes, such as:
[list]
[*] Humans (Society if ... meme (but who tf would wanna play as a human, ew))
[*] Florans (Unga bunga plant people)
[*] Apex (Futuristic tech monkeys)
[*] Avians (Egyptian birds)
[*] Glitch (Ye olde clanker)
[*] Hylotl (Samurai fish)
[*] Novakids (Yee haw star-people)
[/list]

Starbound also has a main story to follow, although it is... Repetitive, to say the least. All you do in said ‘main story’ is:
[olist]
[*] Scan some furniture relating to a race
[*] Do a dungeon with a boss at the end
[*] Enter the next tier of progression (Upgrade your 'pickaxe', get better armor, weapons, tools and crafting stations, etc.)
[/olist]
And repeat that about... 5 times.
Yeah, I'm not really making it seem all that interesting, am I?

Well, the game also has base-building aspects. Which... If I'm already making comparisons to Terraria, well... yeah, no ♥♥♥♥ it's gonna have base-building.
You have two options when it comes to building a base.
Either you work on making your spaceship look nice, or you can build bases on planets.
Unfortunately, you don't get to make your OWN spaceship in vanilla. You get a pre-made, indestructable ship that you can expand using upgrades.
The most you can do with ships is change the background walls, build with some blocks in rooms, and add furniture.

While the gameplay may be lacking in some departments, the music on the other hand, absolutely does NOT fail to deliver.
Some personal recommendations I have are:
[list]
[*] Europa - It sounds so serene, like running your hand across tall grass as your worries become left behind.
[*] Starbound (or Title Menu) - Nostalgic af to me.
[*] The Ruin's Surface (or Crystal Battle 1) - Still gives me chills years later, especially 2:18.
[*] On the Beach at Night - Has to be like... the most iconic Starbound track, ever.
[/list]

So, if you decide to play Starbound, maybe ♥♥♥♥ around in vanilla for a bit to get a feeling for the game, but please for the love of God play modded.
The singular reason why this game's community isn't completely dead is because of the mods.
Speaking of which, Starbound mods are where I first discovered that Protogens exist.
...I was eight, I didn't know any better... I thought they looked cool, man...
(btw, if you want a mod recommendation, I'd suggest Frakin' Universe. It's like the Calamity Mod equivalent for Starbound.)

The vanilla game also has some side-content besides the whole main gameplay loop, such as bounty hunting (which i never did), or some post-game dungeons, but I'd still recommend the modding scene way more.
Even then, I'm still pretty biased towards the game, and will always remember it fondly.
So... Thanks, Starbound.

Space travel? Check.
Story and lore? Check.
Inactive community? No one cares about that. well, except for me.
Replayability? Check.
Basicly checks off all the checkmarks that I put off.
This game was made corrupt devs, so don't buy it. You can read the stories for yourself. If you have to buy this for whatever reason, then you will likely need to mod it to enjoy it.
awesome game tons of active modders the servers are unpopular tho
Great fun, time waste simulator space.
Great cosy game for people out there who want something like Terraria
i never got out of the very early game because of so many different things to do
One of the best space explorations games ever made.

I only wish that Starbound was recognized for the brilliant thing it was. It is a beautiful thing, so damn fun to play, and damn me if you must, but I am a sucker for free-roam sci-fi. It took Terraria and ran with it. I am cheating when I say that, however. I play Starbound with 100 mods, after all. But I touch down on these planets like it is something out of Star Trek, build some hut I am definitely going to forget, use an f-off LAZER to burrow into the planet core, bery bombad of me, I know. And I have a hell of a time doing it. 

Then the modders added insane new hubs, missions, out of this world progression (literally... sorry Earth...), and fun bosses that keep you grinding for better weapons, and praying you get a cool looking new crewmember. I love this game so damn much. I just wish I could play with someone lol... nobody is going to read this so I will leave that here.
insufferable Sjw developers, dont bother playing if man.
just go play terraria man this isnt worth the money in any capacity
great heavily modded, freaky mods can allow for inf money glitch.

yeehaw I'm rootin' tootin' novakid shootin'

red dead redemption or something like that
My favorite crafting game ever!
Very fun to play when modded!
I love this game and it's space traveling
This game is such a slog to play
Games ok, With mods tho? games lovely!
kind of boring for me sadly
10/10 game
